race.name: L'Etape Cancun by Tour de France
race.display_name: L'Etape Cancun by Tour de France
race.tagline: Blaze 63 flat miles of Caribbean speed on closed roads, Tour de France style.

vitals.registration: Tour-branded closed-road ride with finisher perks; costs undisclosed but expect premium entry.
vitals.aid_stations: Standard hydration and fuel on flat rocket roads.
distance_options: Ruta Corta: 34mi, 0ft — breezy intro to paradise pedaling

climate.description: May in Cancun slams 82-90°F days with crushing humidity and steady Caribbean breezes. Low rain risk early month keeps roads dry for speed. Tropical vibes demand smart hydration or you'll melt.

terrain.surface: Silky paved carreteras stretch endlessly straight under palm shade. Closed roads beg for aero tuck and groupo pacchetti. Caribbean views distract from the humid grind—no potholes, just pure velocity.

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: T3 fits this newbie Tour de France tropical transplant—prestige punches above flat weight but lacks Alpine suffering cred. Logistics shine in resort land; adventure pops with beachy exoticism. Still building rep beyond 2024 debut.

logistics.explanation: Cancun International is a dream gateway—direct flights galore, 15-min ride to party central. Shuttles and rentals make start line effortless. Resort sprawl means zero logistical headaches for fly-and-ride bliss. Only gripe: peak season crowds.

length.explanation: 63-mile flagship hits gran fondo sweet spot—not epic brutality, but enough to earn bragging rights. Short 34mi option welcomes newbs without diluting vibe. Lacks century punch for distance junkies. Solid middleweight contender.

technicality.explanation: Dead-straight paved perfection begs pure watts, no cornering IQ needed. Zero tech demands make it idiot-proof fast. Pros: group flying; cons: boredom for bike handlers. Pure power playground.

elevation.explanation: Zilch climbs—flatter than a tortilla. Minimal gain lets speed demons thrive sans suffering. Climbers need not apply; sprinters and rouleurs rejoice. Rare pure-flat Tour de France proxy.

climate.explanation: May heat-haze hell: 90°F sticky sauna with humidity that chokes. Breezes help but demand ice-vest prep. Low rain rocks, but unprepared melt into puddles. Tropical test of mental steel.

altitude.explanation: Sea-level snooze—no thin air drama. Pure oxygen abundance favors raw power over acclimation. Zero hypoxia heroics needed. Flatlanders' dream, hill beasts yawn.

adventure.explanation: Caribbean turquoise dreamscape with cenotes and palms—exotic escape from Euro Alps. Closed roads in paradise scream bucket-list. Tropical Tour twist delivers fresh thrill. Vacation disguised as race.

prestige.explanation: Official Tour de France badge instant cred for amateurs. L'Etape seal elevates beyond local spin. New kid shines via global series glow. Flat factor caps at solid-not-stellar.

race_quality.explanation: Pro-closed roads, Tour branding, finisher swag—polished production. Flat speedfest organized tight. Minimal elevation ups fun quotient. Series pedigree ensures quality crush.

experience.explanation: Tour de France aura on beach cruiser heaven—pure magic. Sea views, breezes, tropical rush unbeatable. Finisher feels epic sans Alps agony. Five stars for sensory overload.

community.explanation: Amateur global mix targets Tour dreamers, but small field lacks depth. Mexico scene building post-2024. Festive vibes unite, prestige draws quality. Growing but not packed.

field_depth.explanation: 1,300+ sister events hint potential, but Cancun newbie lacks pro-level pack. Enthusiast-heavy, not elite showdown. Fun groups, shallow talent pool. Room to grow beasts.

value.explanation: Premium Tour entry for flat fun—decent if you love beach + badge. Inclusions shine, but price likely stings sans climbs. Vacation combo boosts bang. Middling pure-race ROI.

expenses.explanation: Flights to CUN cheapish, resorts wallet-busting peak May. Race fee premium, food/lodging adds up fast. Budget hacks exist via packages. Travel tax tempers appeal.

biased_opinion.summary: L'Etape Cancun flips Tour script with pancake-flat Caribbean crushers—63 miles of wind-whipped speed on closed resort roads. Tour de France prestige crashes tropical party, delivering pro vibes minus masochistic mountains. Humidity bites, but sea views soothe; perfect for power hunters bored of Alps. Newbie series star builds cred fast. Aero tuck and pray for tailwinds.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Power pedaler heaven, climber skip. Tour badge on vacation wheels—worth it for flat fun seekers. Builds series heat in Mexico.

final_verdict.one_liner: Tour glamour meets beach burner—bliss for flat fiends.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you crave Tour cred on flat rockets amid turquoise bliss and hate hills. Skip if humidity horrors or climb quests define your sufferfest. Power up for paradise paceline.
final_verdict.alternatives: Chase Alps in letape-alps or grind Mexico hills in letape-mexico; domestique stateside gran fondos.

logistics.airport: Cancun International (CUN) 15-20km from start—direct global flights swarm. Taxis, shuttles, rentals zip you beachside fast. No-fuss fly-in heaven.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotel Zone resorts steps from Malecón Tajamar start—book early for May peak. All-inclusive packs race + vacay. Budget hostels inland if pinching pesos.
logistics.food: Taco trucks to resort buffets—fuel cheap and flavorful. Pre-race ceviche vibes, post carbs galore. Hydrate heavy in humidity hell.
logistics.packet_pickup: Central Zona Hotelera expo day prior.
logistics.parking: Ample at Malecón start; valet vibes.

history.founder: Tour de France L'Etape Series organizers
history.origin_story: Born 2024 as L'Etape Series' Caribbean debut, bringing Tour's closed-road glory to Mexico's coast. Inaugural smashed with flat speed amid palms, targeting amateurs craving pro aura sans Alps. 2026 edition amps with open regs, three routes in turquoise paradise. Instant hit for tropical twist on gran fondo formula.
history.reputation: Rising star in L'Etape lineup—prestige punch with fun-first flats. Early buzz strong post-debut. Flat format divides purists but hooks speed demons.

course_description.character: Endless straight paved blasts hug Caribbean coast, cenotes flashing by under palm sway. Closed carreteras enable full-gas group flies with sea breezes pushing or punishing. Minimal bumps focus watts on velocity—Tour branding elevates beach cruise to gran fondo glory. Tropical energy fuels non-stop speed till Malecón finish.

suffering_zones.label: Humidity Hammer
suffering_zones.desc: Mid-route heat peaks crush souls as humidity spikes. Breezes tease relief but demand constant sipping. Mental melt zone tests resolve.

course_description.signature_challenge: Caribbean crosswinds on exposed flats—gusts shred pacelines, reward aero savvy. No climbs, all endurance bake in 90°F steam. Speed survival defines the suffer.

guide_variables.race_name: L'Etape Cancun by Tour de France

non_negotiables.why: Flat speed demands sustained power in humidity furnace. Builds heat tolerance for 6+ hour bake. Paceline proficiency key to surviving wind-whipped groups.
